ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for wholemeal bread with cheese as a composite dish; mapped to bread food product as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products; no specific code for wholemeal bread with cheese composite. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this composite bread-and-cheese dish.

serving.description: 2 slices wholemeal bread with 1–2 slices cheese (~70g)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 70g, comprising two slices of wholemeal bread topped or sandwiched with one to two slices of processed or natural cheese.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal bread with cheese — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for wholemeal bread with cheese (cr_id F05020134). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — wholemeal / whole wheat bread analogous entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 69 (medium) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for wholemeal bread, which is the primary carbohydrate component of this dish. The cheese component contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (69) × carbohydrate per serving (26.85g × 0.70 = 18.8g) / 100 = 13.0, rounded to 12.9 using the HPB-reported carbohydrate value and 70g serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 712mg/100g — exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this food due to its very high sodium content (712mg/100g), primarily from the cheese; if consumed, restrict to one thin slice of cheese on a single slice of wholemeal bread and balance with low-sodium foods throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (69) with moderate carbohydrate (26.85g/100g), partially offset by high protein (16.68g/100g) and dietary fibre (3.16g/100g) which slow glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may include this food in moderate portions (60–80g per serving) as the high protein and fibre content attenuate the glycaemic response; monitor post-meal blood glucose and pair with non-starchy vegetables to further reduce glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (712mg/100g) and saturated fat (~3.2g/100g) are cardiovascular risk factors, partially offset by wholegrain fibre (3.16g/100g) and high calcium (814mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should choose low-sodium, reduced-fat cheese variants and limit portion size to one serving (~70g) to manage sodium and saturated fat intake, while benefiting from the wholegrain fibre content which supports LDL cholesterol reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High calcium (814mg/100g) supports bone protection in corticosteroid-treated RA patients; however, sodium at 712mg/100g may exacerbate tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients on corticosteroids may benefit from the high calcium content for bone protection, but should choose low-sodium cheese and limit portion size to reduce sodium-driven inflammation risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholemeal bread with cheese is a simple, nutritious bread-based dish consisting of sliced wholemeal (whole wheat) bread paired with sliced processed or natural cheese. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or light meal in Singapore and across Western-influenced food cultures. The wholemeal bread provides complex carbohydrates, dietary fibre (3.16g/100g), and iron (3.82mg/100g), while the cheese contributes high protein (16.68g/100g) and exceptional calcium (814mg/100g), making this combination nutritionally dense. The texture is soft and slightly chewy from the wholemeal bread, with a creamy, mildly salty flavour from the cheese. Clinically, this food is notable for its very high sodium content (712mg/100g), which makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension without significant portion control or substitution with low-sodium cheese. For Type 2 Diabetes management, the medium glycaemic index (GI 69) is partially mitigated by the high protein and fibre content. The saturated fat content (~3.2g/100g) is moderate and within acceptable limits for cardiovascular health when consumed in reasonable portions. This food is vegetarian and halal-suitable, and is a practical source of calcium and iron for the general population.

Calories in wholemeal bread with cheese
Is wholemeal bread with cheese good for diabetics?
Sodium content of wholemeal bread with cheese
Can I eat wholemeal bread with cheese if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id F05020134) with confidence 0.95. GI value of 69 for wholemeal bread is well-established in the University of Sydney GI Database. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at approximately 3.2g/100g based on typical cheese-to-bread ratio for this dish type; the HPB record does not explicitly report saturated fat, so this value is an approximation consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for similar wholemeal bread and processed cheese combinations. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this composite dish and are not available from a single approved source for this exact formulation. Glycemic load per serving calculated using 70g serving size and HPB carbohydrate value: GL = 69 × (26.85 × 0.70) / 100 ≈ 12.9. The food is classified as a Singaporean-context food record sourced from HPB; multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per G6 Singaporean dish requirements.
